sections.name: Understanding the organization and its context
sections.description: The organization shall determine external and internal issues relevant to its purpose that affect its ability to achieve the intended outcomes of the BCMS.

sections.name: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
sections.description: The organization shall determine relevant interested parties and their applicable business continuity requirements, including legal and regulatory obligations.

sections.name: Determining the scope of the business continuity management system
sections.description: The organization shall determine the boundaries and applicability of the BCMS considering context and requirements, and define and document its scope.

sections.name: Business continuity management system
sections.description: The organization shall establish, implement, maintain, and continually improve a BCMS, including required processes and their interactions.

sections.name: Leadership and commitment
sections.description: Top management shall demonstrate leadership and commitment to the BCMS, ensuring alignment with strategic direction, resource provision, and continual improvement.

sections.name: Policy
sections.description: Top management shall establish, implement, maintain, and communicate a business continuity policy appropriate to the organization and aligned to objectives.

sections.name: Roles, responsibilities and authorities
sections.description: Top management shall ensure that BCMS responsibilities and authorities are assigned and communicated, including accountability for conformity and performance reporting.

sections.name: Actions to address risks and opportunities
sections.description: The organization shall determine and plan actions to address risks and opportunities that affect the BCMS’s ability to achieve intended outcomes.

sections.name: Business continuity objectives and planning to achieve them
sections.description: The organization shall establish measurable business continuity objectives and plan actions, resources, and evaluation methods to achieve them.

sections.name: Planning changes to the business continuity management system
Resources
sections.description: The organization shall determine and provide resources necessary for the establishment, implementation, maintenance, and improvement of the BCMS.

sections.name: Competence
sections.description: The organization shall ensure personnel performing BCMS-related roles are competent and retain documented evidence of competence.

sections.name: Awareness
sections.description: The organization shall ensure personnel are aware of the BCMS policy, their roles, contributions to effectiveness, and implications of nonconformance.

sections.name: Communication
sections.description: The organization shall determine and manage internal and external communications relevant to the BCMS.

sections.name: Documented information
sections.description: The organization shall determine, create, update, and control documented information required by the BCMS and ISO 22301.

sections.name: Operational planning and control
sections.description: The organization shall plan, implement, and control processes needed to meet BCMS requirements and manage changes.

sections.name: Business impact analysis and risk assessment
sections.description: The organization shall establish and maintain systematic processes for conducting business impact analysis and disruption risk assessment.

sections.name: Business continuity strategies and solutions
sections.description: The organization shall identify, select, implement, and maintain business continuity strategies and solutions based on BIA and risk assessment outputs.

sections.name: Business continuity plans and procedures
sections.description: The organization shall establish and maintain response structures, communication procedures, and business continuity plans to manage disruptions and recovery.

sections.name: Exercise programme
sections.description: The organization shall implement and maintain an exercise and testing programme to validate business continuity strategies and plans.

sections.name: Evaluation of business continuity documentation and capabilities
sections.description: The organization shall evaluate the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of BC documentation and continuity capabilities.

sections.name: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
sections.description: The organization shall monitor, measure, analyze, and evaluate BCMS performance and retain documented results.

sections.name: Internal audit
sections.description: The organization shall conduct internal audits and establish an audit programme to assess BCMS conformity and effectiveness.

sections.name: Management review
sections.description: Top management shall review the BCMS at planned intervals, considering required inputs and documenting outputs and improvement actions.b

sections.name: Nonconformity and corrective action
sections.description: The organization shall manage nonconformities, implement corrective actions, review effectiveness, and retain documented evidence.

sections.name: Continual improvement
sections.description: The organization shall continually improve the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the BCMS.
